heading: Powers and duties of the Authority
label: 7105 Powers and duties of the Authority

children.text: The Authority shall provide leadership in establishing policies and guidance relating to matters under this chapter, and, except as otherwise provided, shall be responsible for carrying out the purpose of this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: determine the appropriateness of units for labor organization representation under ;section 7112 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: supervise or conduct elections to determine whether a labor organization has been selected as an exclusive representative by a majority of the employees in an appropriate unit and otherwise administer the provisions of relating to the according of exclusive recognition to labor organizations;section 7111 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: prescribe criteria and resolve issues relating to the granting of national consultation rights under ;section 7113 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: prescribe criteria and resolve issues relating to determining compelling need for agency rules or regulations under ;section 7117(b) of this title

children.label: E
children.text: resolves issues relating to the duty to bargain in good faith under ;section 7117(c) of this title

children.label: F
children.text: prescribe criteria relating to the granting of consultation rights with respect to conditions of employment under ;section 7117(d) of this title

children.label: G
children.text: conduct hearings and resolve complaints of unfair labor practices under ;section 7118 of this title

children.label: H
children.text: resolve exceptions to arbitrator’s awards under ; andsection 7122 of this title

children.label: I
children.text: take such other actions as are necessary and appropriate to effectively administer the provisions of this chapter.

children.label: b
children.text: The Authority shall adopt an official seal which shall be judicially noticed.

children.label: c
children.text: The principal office of the Authority shall be in or about the District of Columbia, but the Authority may meet and exercise any or all of its powers at any time or place. Except as otherwise expressly provided by law, the Authority may, by one or more of its members or by such agents as it may designate, make any appropriate inquiry necessary to carry out its duties wherever persons subject to this chapter are located. Any member who participates in the inquiry shall not be disqualified from later participating in a decision of the Authority in any case relating to the inquiry.

children.label: d
children.text: The Authority shall appoint an Executive Director and such regional directors, administrative law judges under , and other individuals as it may from time to time find necessary for the proper performance of its functions. The Authority may delegate to officers and employees appointed under this subsection authority to perform such duties and make such expenditures as may be necessary.section 3105 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: to determine whether a group of employees is an appropriate unit;

children.label: B
children.text: to conduct investigations and to provide for hearings;

children.label: C
children.text: to determine whether a question of representation exists and to direct an election; and

children.label: D
children.text: to supervise or conduct secret ballot elections and certify the results thereof.

The Authority may delegate to any administrative law judge appointed under subsection (d) of this section its authority under to determine whether any person has engaged in or is engaging in an unfair labor practice.section 7118 of this title
the date of the action; or
the date of the filing of any application under this subsection for review of the action;
hold hearings;
administer oaths, take the testimony or deposition of any person under oath, and issue subpenas as provided in ; andsection 7132 of this title

may require an agency or a labor organization to cease and desist from violations of this chapter and require it to take any remedial action it considers appropriate to carry out the policies of this chapter.

children.label: h
children.text: Except as provided in , relating to litigation before the Supreme Court, attorneys designated by the Authority may appear for the Authority and represent the Authority in any civil action brought in connection with any function carried out by the Authority pursuant to this title or as otherwise authorized by law.section 518 of title 28

children.label: i
children.text: In the exercise of the functions of the Authority under this title, the Authority may request from the Director of the Office of Personnel Management an advisory opinion concerning the proper interpretation of rules, regulations, or policy directives issued by the Office of Personnel Management in connection with any matter before the Authority.
